name: Continue The Active PTX One-K 128x128 Control-Path Exploit

summary.name: Continue The Active PTX One-K 128x128 Control-Path Exploit
summary.hypothesis: The latest run is 24.634880 ms against an accepted anchor of 24.164272 ms. Round round 94/100 should replay the bounded `control_path` family once from the current state so the search keeps advancing instead of restoring forever.
summary.expected_bottleneck: A branch-local hot-band scheduling or geometry bottleneck on the dominant kernel path, rather than a pure restore-only action.
summary.risk: Moderate. This branch stays on the PTX winner surface but its upside is measured in hundredths of a millisecond and the family is easy to over-replay.
ranking_notes: Replay the `control_path` family as an exact historical surface to keep the loop bounded and auditable.
Keep restore as the deterministic fallback behind any replay branch.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if this replay does not beat the active anchor cleanly or if it broadens beyond an exact bounded historical surface.

notes: Node C may now implement this one candidate.
